advantages: Highly interpretable: each prediction follows an explicit chain of human-readable if-then rules from root to leaf.
Handles nonlinear interactions and mixed feature types without manual feature crosses — splits discover local structure automatically.
No feature scaling required: threshold splits are rank-invariant for monotonic transforms of individual features.
Fast inference: prediction cost is O(tree depth), independent of training set size once the tree is built.
Minimal preprocessing for numeric features — no distributional assumption like Gaussian class-conditional densities.
disadvantages: High variance: small training changes can flip early splits and produce very different trees — unstable alone on small data.
Greedy splits are locally optimal, not globally optimal — the best first split may block better overall structure.
Axis-aligned boundaries cannot represent diagonal or smooth decision surfaces efficiently — many splits needed for oblique separation.
Overfits easily without depth, leaf-size, or pruning constraints — training accuracy can reach 100% while validation fails.
Biased toward features with many distinct values or high cardinality when impurity is evaluated naively — importance and split preference skew.
